description: Manual identity mappings for tokens whose TLA snapshot names are raw addresses (cw20:terra1... or native:factory/...). When the snapshot cron can't resolve a gauge entry to a friendly symbol, it stores the raw address as the pool name. This file lets the dashboard (and any other consumer) substitute a friendly name for known cases. Keys must match the snapshot name EXACTLY including the cw20: or native: prefix.
instructions: To add a token: (1) Verify identity via chain query, project docs, or DEX listing. (2) Add an entry to the `tokens` object below with the snapshot name as key and the friendly display name as value. (3) Commit to this repo. Pages will pick up the new mapping on next reload.
notes: The two _example_ entries above are placeholders. Remove them once real entries exist; the loader silently ignores keys starting with underscore.
Known unresolved entries seen so far (need identification):

cw20:terra1hqq6pnx74q0wae7eqqu2wgddzr2xn8l9gvd33sf  — possibly wBTC.credia.c (~423K VP, source_type=cw20, in_staking_contract=true). Address has invalid bech32 checksum so chain query fails. Needs manual confirmation.
cw20:terra10xwhrfj2hqtwr4jwam3e24w92ypgl85lqz35lqz  — zero VP, status=zero_vp (inactive). Same invalid-bech32 issue.
